items.content: Ran cambium's first real end-to-end knowledge cycle on balatron history (distill -> recall -> promote to team -> endorse -> org PR -> cross-project recall). Federation validated: balatron-earned knowledge recalled from the Clark project.
items.why: Drove cambium_server.py functions directly (the cambium MCP tools were not exposed in the session) with CAMBIUM_REPO=balatron, CAMBIUM_ORG_REPO=knowledge, org PR mode on. distill() produced 80 items (62 decisions + 18 constraints) entirely from context-keeper; agentsync contributed 0 (see companion constraint). Five standing recall queries, run to cross the recalls>=3 gate, promoted 22 items to the team cambium branch via the worktree+CAS path. con-016 (doc-sync) and con-014 (regime-boundary) were endorsed as universal practice and promoted to org as PRs #1/#2 (since merged). Pointing cambium at the Clark repo with the same org store, both items recalled back stamped project=balatron â€” the pass condition for compound, cross-project knowledge growth.

items.content: To distill agentsync outcomes for a project, that project's OWN origin must carry the agentsync coordination branch. distill() reads origin/<agentsync_branch> of CAMBIUM_REPO only â€” it will not reach a board hosted on a different repo, and released claims are never reconstructed from history.
items.why: In balatron's first cycle distill() returned 0 agentsync items and reported 'no coordination branch found', because balatron's origin has no agentsync branch â€” the team's live board lives on agentsync-remote/aethermoor, and all of balatron's claims had already been released. Cambium silently falls back to context-keeper only, so you lose every agentsync outcome-memory without any error. Verify the branch exists (git ls-remote --heads origin agentsync) before trusting a distill's agentsync count.

items.content: In PR-mode org promotion, promote items to org ONE AT A TIME â€” merge each cambium promotion PR before opening the next. Do not open multiple org promotion PRs against knowledge.json concurrently.
items.why: Each promote(item_id, to_scope='org') branches off origin/main and appends to the same knowledge.json items array. Two concurrently-open promotion PRs edit the same region of that array, so the second cannot merge cleanly until the first lands â€” this is the documented non-transactional PR-mode limitation. Serializing (open PR, merge, then promote the next) avoids the conflict entirely.
